metadata.title: SYNGO_CC_4755
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Figure 3: immunohistochemistry localized AP3M1/2 (antibody is aspecific) to recycling endosomes (RE), early endosomes (EE) and lysosomes in both cell body and dendrites. Highest co-localization is with RE.

Figure 4: shRNA knockdown of of AP3M1 (μ3A) specifically strongly reduced dendritic AP3M1/2 puncta (since the antibody is aspecific, remaining staining is probably AP3M2/μ3B). This knockdown also affected surface-expressed GluA2.

Figure 6: AP3M1 (μ3A-FLAG) is necessary and sufficient to recruit GluA2 to recycling endosomes and enhances the ability of GRIP1 to recruit GluA2 to the dendritic surface. Experimental description: "The HsCt5 mouse was generated as part of a lentiviral enhancer trap screen"

antibody:
"A rabbit anti-μ3 polyclonal antibody was raised against the peptide DMYGEKYKPFKGVKY (LifeTein, residues 393-407) by Cocalico Biologicals. Immunohistochemical staining was abolished by preabsorption with the peptide used to generate the antibody (Figure S3C) and was decreased when mu3A was knocked down by RNAi (Figures 4A-4C)."

protein blast of this peptide is 100% match with mouse Ap3m1, 93% match with mouse Ap3m2.

"Both shRNAs reduced the intensity of μ3 staining in the somatic and dendritic compartments to ~60% and ~40% of control, respectively (Figures 4A and 4B, n = 9-13 neurons/condition, p < 0.05 for soma, p < 0.01 for dendrite). This likely represents an underestimate of the degree of μ3A KD, because the shRNAs are specific for μ3A but the antibody recognizes both μ3A and μ3B (Figure S3B)."
